assumptions: The CSV was interpreted as a two-column dataset: 'Date' and 'Website_Visits', with values provided in alternating order after the header.
Zero website visits (0) on 2025-09-05 and 2025-09-09 are treated as anomalous/outage values rather than true traffic and thus flagged as outliers.
Very high spikes of 2500 (2025-09-15) and 5545 (2025-09-18) are considered outliers relative to the typical 480–620 range and removed from the cleaned dataset.
Outlier detection is based on domain-judgment thresholds (near-constant range around ~500 visits) rather than a strictly formal statistical rule, due to the small sample size.
No date parsing errors were assumed; all dates are valid and unique, so no deduplication or date correction was required.
No imputation was performed for removed outliers; the cleaned dataset simply excludes those rows.
Descriptive statistics are computed strictly on the cleaned dataset of 16 rows, excluding all detected outliers.
validation_notes: Outlier Removal Check: Passed. All values identified as outliers (0, 0, 2500, 5545) appear only in 'detected_outliers' and 'removed_rows' and are absent from 'cleaned_data_rows'.
Statistical Validity Check: Passed. The statistics (count=16, mean=524.8125, median=521.5, std≈37.2907, min=488, max=621) were recomputed using only the 16 cleaned Website_Visits values.
The sum of cleaned Website_Visits values is 8397; dividing by 16 yields the reported mean of 524.8125, confirming consistency.
Median was computed as the average of the 8th and 9th sorted cleaned values (521 and 522), giving 521.5.
Standard deviation was calculated on the cleaned dataset only, using the sample standard deviation formula.
